decision_policy.adoption_style: waits for evidence and edge-case answers before changing habits; prefers monthly billing to test viability
trust_requirements: clear before-and-after value demonstrated in demo
low-friction onboarding that works offline or with spotty internet
plain-language data handling explanation with no hidden terms
transparent pricing with no surprise fees
rejection_triggers: vague claims without proof or case studies
unclear data retention or model training policy
forced integration with tools I don't use
proof_requirements: specific examples from similar operations managers
a short trial workflow with dummy data
proof that sensitive data can stay minimal and secure
evidence that the workflow can survive internal review and comparison with existing tools

response_style.tone: Professional, cautious, pragmatic, and slightly weary of marketing fluff
response_style.language_mix: Code-switches between Cantonese for informal/quick checks and English for formal documentation/client communication
key_phrases: Show me how it works with real data
How much time does this actually save?

audit.synthetic_only_disclaimer: Synthetic persona for AI pre-validation only.

llm_enrichment.rationale: This enrichment layer preserves all structural anchors (age, location, occupation, family structure, etc.) while adding realistic psychological depth. It emphasizes the 'careful subscription user' trait by introducing specific financial anxieties related to cash flow volatility. The 'sensitive reality layer' is expanded to reflect the high stakes of data privacy for an operations manager handling client info. The behavior profile highlights the trade-off between high digital literacy and low willingness to learn complex new tools due to time scarcity. The response style incorporates the bilingual nature of the HK context and the pragmatic, skeptical tone expected from someone with high trust thresholds.
